title: Cross-Asset Tail Dependence & Contagion Analysis

what_i_noticed: Cycle 37 added single-asset tail risk metrics (Sortino, Omega, Calmar) but there was zero cross-asset dependence analysis
Portfolio engine uses Ledoit-Wolf covariance (Pearson correlation) which assumes elliptical dependence — this completely misses tail dependence
Under Gaussian copula (implicit in Pearson correlation), tail dependence is exactly 0 for any correlation < 1. Real equity markets have substantial lower tail dependence (co-crashes)
Bloomberg has CORR screens but systematic empirical copula-based tail dependence with contagion scoring is institutional-grade analytics that most retail platforms lack
All 12 stock signals returned 'Buy' with identical crash probs — a separate issue, but tail dependence helps expose hidden portfolio concentration risk that the signal engine misses
observation.gap_identified: No measurement of how assets co-crash. A portfolio could show 'good diversification' by Pearson correlation while having severe tail risk concentration (e.g., SPY-QQQ: ρ=0.88, λ_L=0.63)

implementation.what_i_built: Full cross-asset tail dependence engine using empirical copula methods
key_features: Empirical lower tail dependence λ_L via Frahm-Junker-Schmidt (2005) estimator, averaged over quantile grid [0.02, 0.10] for robustness
Upper tail dependence λ_U for asymmetry detection (equities typically have λ_L > λ_U)
Contagion score: excess tail dependence beyond what Pearson correlation predicts (under Gaussian copula, λ=0 for ρ<1, so any positive λ is 'hidden' risk)
Cluster detection via BFS on high-tail-dep adjacency graph — identifies groups of assets that co-crash
Rolling tail dependence time series for regime shift detection (e.g., tail dep spikes before crises)
Portfolio-level diversification quality rating (excellent/good/fair/poor) based on aggregate tail dep

theory.tail_dependence: λ_L = lim(u→0) P(U₁ ≤ u | U₂ ≤ u) where U_i are probability integral transforms
theory.empirical_estimator: λ̂_L(q) = P(U₁ ≤ q, U₂ ≤ q) / q, averaged over q ∈ [0.02, 0.10]
theory.why_not_parametric: No parametric copula assumption (Clayton, Gumbel) — empirical approach is more robust and makes no distributional assumption
references: Frahm, Junker & Schmidt (2005) 'Estimating the tail-dependence coefficient'
Embrechts, McNeil & Straumann (2002) 'Correlation and dependence in risk management'
Ang & Chen (2002) 'Asymmetric correlations of equity portfolios' (JFE)

SPY_QQQ.interpretation: Very high co-crash risk — diversification fails in tail events

assessment.why: Fills a genuine gap — no existing cross-asset dependence analysis. The engine now measures something that Pearson correlation fundamentally cannot: how much diversification benefit disappears in crashes. This is what prop desks and institutional risk teams monitor but retail platforms never show.
limitations: Empirical estimator needs ~500+ observations for stable estimates at q=0.02
No parametric copula fitting (could add Clayton/Gumbel for extrapolation below observed quantiles)
No integration yet with portfolio optimizer (could penalize high-tail-dep allocations)
Frontend component not built yet — results only available via API

next_steps: Build frontend component to visualize tail dependence heatmap and contagion clusters
Integrate tail dependence into portfolio optimizer (penalize concentrated tail risk)
Add parametric copula fitting (Clayton for lower tail, Gumbel for upper) for comparison
Wire tail dependence warnings into signal engine (flag portfolios with high hidden contagion)
Add stress-conditional tail dependence (compute λ_L only during VIX > 25 periods)
